inputs.fcf_source: Normalized consolidated owner cash of approximately $139M divided by 77.017M economic units; bracketed by FY2025 FCF of $120M and Q1 2026 annualized FCF of $164M.
inputs.shares_source: Q1 2026 10-Q: 27,839,229 Class A plus 49,177,775 Class B/OpCo units
inputs.book_per_share_caveat: Class A stockholders equity $340.3M; land largely in intangibles at acquisition cost — not a fair-value dhando floor for 315k+ acres
inputs.normalization_note: Up-C structure: Lawrence base uses Class A-attributable owner cash, not consolidated FCF per Class A share without NCI adjustment. Q1 2026 run-rate higher ($40.9M consolidated FCF) — not used as FCF₀.

optionality_gate.floor_value_note: Conservative $4,000/acre on ~315k surface acres [Assumption]; acquisitions capitalized in intangibles; bolt-ons at higher $/acre
optionality_gate.overlay_nav_note: Illustrative $6,000/acre on 315k acres + cash; price $73 embeds premium to conservative NAV overlay
optionality_gate.notes: Unlike TPL 1888 zero-book pattern, LB marks acquired land in intangibles — but GAAP book (~$12/sh) still understates Delaware acreage scarcity.

gaap_vs_fair_value.policy_source: 10-K FY2025: fee surface acres capitalized through acquisitions; not full portfolio mark-to-market each quarter
gaap_vs_fair_value.source: 10-K FY2025 Item 1 Business (own or manage 315k+ acres Dec 31, 2025)

not_in_model_requires_refresh: Digital infrastructure / data-center easement pipeline sizing beyond press releases
Per-acre marks on 2026 bolt-on acquisitions (~5,700 acres Q1 2026)
Full related-party revenue normalization vs WaterBridge (WBI) ecosystem

segments_or_options.label: Digital infrastructure / data-center surface option
segments_or_options.evidence: 10-K and Q1 2026 PR cite digital infrastructure opportunity; no SEC-sized contract in base FCF

segments_or_options.label: Undeveloped Delaware acreage (incremental easements)
segments_or_options.nav_assumption: Incremental $/acre above producing run-rate; 450 new easements FY2025 per 8-K

segments.label: Surface use royalties (incl. related party)
segments.owner_cash_source: FY2025 surface royalties $72.8M × 41.6% attribution ÷ 27.838M shares; ~89% cash conversion [Assumption]
segments.evidence: 8-K FY2025 revenue table; WaterBridge Stateline AMI drives related-party surface fees

segments.label: Resource sales, resource royalties, oil and gas royalties
segments.owner_cash_source: FY2025 combined resource/O&G lines $60.3M × 41.6% attribution ÷ shares
segments.evidence: 8-K FY2025 revenue table; more cyclical than easement base

options.label: Digital infrastructure option
options.base_terminal_rationale: Pre-revenue at scale in SEC filings — explicit zero terminal; sized in nav_overlay
options.evidence: Q1 2026 earnings PR cites digital infrastructure; no base FCF embed

corporate_drag.note: G&A and interest burden on Class A share; FY2025 G&A $62.4M consolidated (10-K)

bear.notes: Permian slowdown, related-party pressure, and no digital conversion.

base.notes: Capital-light surface cash grows with the 2026 guide; options remain outside owner cash.

bull.notes: Water, pore space, and digital leases convert while leverage declines.

option_scan.question: GAAP book misstates core assets?
option_scan.evidence: 315k acres vs $340M Class A equity (~$12/sh); intangibles $137M at acquisition cost not full scarcity value (10-K FY2025)

option_scan.question: Undeveloped reserves / acreage / dormant royalty?
option_scan.evidence: Active land management; 450 new easements FY2025; digital infrastructure cited in Q1 2026 PR

option_scan.question: Backlog not in FCF path?
option_scan.evidence: 2026 Adj EBITDA outlook raised; digital/data-center pipeline not in Lawrence FCF₀

option_scan.question: Private / illiquid stakes below fair value?
option_scan.evidence: Operating land company, not holdco of marks

option_scan.question: Embedded product option already in revenue?
option_scan.evidence: WaterBridge-related surface royalties already in surface segment growth

growth_explanation.mechanism: More easements and surface royalties on 315k Delaware acres as WaterBridge/WBI infrastructure and E&P activity expand; bolt-on acquisitions add acreage.
growth_explanation.base_growth_assumption: 8% years 1-5 on normalized Class A FCF — below mgmt EBITDA guide to avoid double-count

theory_implied.derivation: segment_build owner-cash weighted blend (growth only; exit from scenarios.base)

marvin_floor.notes: Class A-attributable FY2025 owner cash; 10-K / 8-K FY2025

blended_best.notes: Lawrence gate unchanged; Groundbreaker supports Permian easement/digital-infra watch

paths.label: Marvin floor (filings stress)
paths.weight_why: Stress floor from primary docs; anchors pessimistic bound.

paths.label: Blended best estimate (Marvin + external)
paths.notes: Lawrence gate unchanged; Groundbreaker supports Permian easement/digital-infra watch
paths.weight_why: Primary holding-period path after partial credit for approved external normalization.

paths.label: Bear scenario (Lawrence envelope)
paths.weight_why: Downside stress; low weight because bear is a sensitivity, not a competing explanation of base mechanics.
paths.hard_to_vary: yes: asymmetric low weight is structural
weight_falsifiers: Bear assumptions become base case in filings (e.g. sustained volume collapse)

paths.label: Bull scenario (Lawrence envelope)
paths.weight_why: Upside stress probe; higher than bear because optionality archetype has skew, but still below primary paths.
weight_falsifiers: Bull growth sustained in filings for 4+ quarters without falsifier trigger

qualitative_adjustments.rationale: Water segment or index cannot replicate subsurface exposure
qualitative_adjustments.falsifier: Water economics fully in segment exit multiple

deutsch_checks.reach: Weights encode evidence tier (A primary → D external), not fitted to hit a target IRR

synthesis.notes: Capstone IRR combining filings, segments, overlays, third-party, qualitative

context_overlay.disclaimer: Context only. Tailwinds inform stance and overlay sizing; they do not auto-inflate Lawrence base IRR. Promotion to base case requires [HUMAN REVIEW].

themes.label: AI compute -> power demand -> grid/water constraint -> scarce Permian surface

indicators.label: TPL water segment revenue (USD m, FY)

peer_context.note: Context only; percentile rank not in base IRR.

lens_consensus.disclaimer: Advisory lens blend only. Does not replace Lawrence total-synthesis stance gate without [HUMAN REVIEW].

total_return_panel.error: Vendor events are discovery evidence; primary-source completeness has not been established.
total_return_panel.return_contract: split-adjusted close excluding dividends; event amounts normalized for later splits; cash reinvested at ex-date close; pre-tax nominal

coverage.note: Vendor events are discovery evidence; primary-source completeness has not been established.

components.label: Current surface, resource, and royalty fee engine

valuation.evidence: 2026 adjusted EBITDA guidance is $210M-$230M; values use $180M at 10x, $220M at 15x, and $240M at 20x.
valuation.cross_check: Q1 2026 revenue $51.0M, EBITDA $44.9M, FCF $40.9M; do not separately add currently monetized acreage.
valuation.assumption_summary: Enterprise value before net debt; the base multiple is deliberately generous for scarce, capital-light acreage.

valuation.evidence: Q1 2026 long-term debt was approximately $535M; year-end cash was $30.7M.
valuation.cross_check: Base approximates $505M net debt divided by 77.017M economic units.

valuation.evidence: LandBridge purchases ranged from approximately $875 per acre for an 800-acre bolt-on to roughly $7,100 per fee acre in the 1918 Ranch allocation. Only an illustrative 20% of 320k acres is separately marked after overlap and realization discounts.
valuation.cross_check: The remaining acreage and all current revenue are embedded in the fee-engine valuation.

valuation.evidence: PowerBridge holds an option over up to 3,400 acres; first power is targeted for 2027 and scaled generation for 2028. Powered land is scarce, but customer, rent, capital, and enforceable full-campus economics remain undisclosed.
valuation.cross_check: The base remains below $250M across all economic units; no success-case campus value is treated as current contracted rent.

components.label: Pore space and other new-use options

valuation.evidence: Contiguous acreage may support pore space, power, fiber, water, and other industrial uses. The 1918 Ranch and Intrepid South Ranch transactions establish that these rights have non-zero value even before full monetization.
valuation.cross_check: Options graduate into current operations only after contracts establish economics.

component_valuation_results.decision_rule: Use the complete low/base/high component schedule. Embedded components are estimated but not added twice.

pricing_analysis.economic_claim_note: Each listed Class A share represents one OpCo unit. Valuation therefore uses 27.839 million Class A plus 49.178 million exchangeable Class B/OpCo units, rather than dividing consolidated cash flow by Class A shares alone. Sponsor control, tax distributions, and related-party WaterBridge transactions remain governance risks.
pricing_analysis.implied_growth_contract: Constant 7-year owner-cash growth with a 18x terminal owner-cash multiple; diagnostic, not forecast.
pricing_analysis.strongest_counter_explanation: LandBridge may be evolving from an oilfield surface landlord into a uniquely advantaged power, water, pore-space, and digital-infrastructure platform whose recurring economics deserve a multiple above the base case.
falsifiers: Alpha Digital discloses contracted rent or royalty economics that independently add more than $8 per economic unit.
Arm's-length revenue excluding WaterBridge compounds above 12% while net leverage declines.
Acquisitions continue below 8x forward EBITDA without equity dilution or related-party leakage.
pricing_analysis.pricing_conclusion: LandBridge has scarce Delaware Basin acreage and a capital-light fee model. Actual purchases validate roughly $0.9k-$7.1k per acre and approximately 7.6x EBITDA for cash-flowing packages, while its current fee engine already receives a premium 15x base multiple. After improving the dormant-acreage and powered-land option marks, $78.12 remains well above base value but is close to the high economic case. The result is a watch, with the market already paying for successful digital-infrastructure conversion and continued acquisition accretion.

economic_value_bridge.gaap_role: Reference only. Acquisition accounting records recent land closer to transaction value, but legacy acreage, pore space, water, and assembled contiguity are not represented by consolidated book value.
economic_value_bridge.accounting_reference: The 2025 10-K allocated recent purchases among land, surface-use agreements, and brackish-water assets; these allocations are cross-checks, not the valuation conclusion.

component_groups.label: Current surface, resource, and royalty fee engine
component_groups.valuation_basis: $180M/$220M/$240M EBITDA at 10x/15x/20x; the base is materially above the approximately 7.6x multiple paid for LandBridge's cash-flowing Q4 2024 acreage packages.
component_groups.overlap_control: Currently monetized acreage, water royalties, and surface-use contracts are embedded here and not added as full land NAV.

component_groups.label: Net debt and financing claims
component_groups.valuation_basis: Balance-sheet claim across all 77.017M economic units.

component_groups.label: Dormant and under-monetized acreage
component_groups.valuation_basis: Only an illustrative 20% of 320,000 acres receives a separate risked mark against actual $0.9k-$7.1k per-acre purchases.
component_groups.overlap_control: The remaining acreage is embedded in the fee engine.

component_groups.label: Alpha Digital powered-land option
component_groups.valuation_basis: Milestone option on up to 3,400 acres and 2 GW; powered-land scarcity raises success value, but undisclosed rent and customer terms keep the probability-weighted base modest.
component_groups.overlap_control: No full campus value is capitalized before customer, power, and lease economics are contractual.

component_groups.label: Pore space, water, fiber, and other new uses
component_groups.valuation_basis: Portfolio option reserve informed by the 1918 Ranch allocation and Intrepid South Ranch water/land transaction.
component_groups.overlap_control: Existing WaterBridge-related cash flow stays in the fee engine.

wisdom_applied: Horizon Kinetics: prioritize the 80% free-cash-flow-margin royalty and toll structure over GAAP land carrying value.
Groundbreaker: contiguous powered land and local water are scarce physical bottlenecks, but first-order power enthusiasm may already be priced.
Klarman: transaction-backed land value and uncontracted catalysts are shown separately.
Marks: sponsor control, related-party revenue, and leverage remain claims against realization value.
limitations: LandBridge does not disclose Alpha Digital rent, customer commitments, or full powered-land economics.
Per-acre purchases include different mixes of fee title, leasehold, water, pore space, and existing contracts and cannot be applied uniformly.
The 20% dormant-acreage split is provisional pending parcel-level revenue attribution.
